@NikolayS — open-source digest March 15 – March 21, 2026
Vol. 1, No. 2 github.com/NikolayS
the dispatch
@NikolayS
Four releases, one AI agent with loose lips, and a dental office buzzer walks into a repo.
236 commits 64 PRs 6 releases 7 repos

Eighty-seven commits to rpg alone, four releases across two repos, and one API key that briefly saw the light of day.

RELEASE

rpg v0.8.0 Ships EXPLAIN Visualization That Actually Explains Things

The Rust-based Postgres terminal gets colorized plan output, automatic issue detection, one-command plan sharing, and Lua scripting in a single week that also saw v0.5.0 through v0.7.0.

Four releases in five days. @NikolayS burned through versions 0.5.0 to 0.8.0, each stacking features like a DBA stacks indexes on a troubled table. The headline act in v0.8.0: enhanced EXPLAIN visualization with a summary header, hot-path highlighting, automatic warnings for sequential scans on large tables, row estimate errors over 10x, and sorts spilling to disk. Plans can be shared to explain.depesz.com, explain.dalibo.com, or pgMustard in a single command. Lua scripting landed too — user-defined slash commands via rpg.query() and friends, gated behind a --features lua build flag. Earlier in the week, v0.6.0 and v0.7.0 locked down the /ask AI path with read-only transaction wrapping (#670), while v0.5.0 added multi-category SQL syntax highlighting and auto-detection of AI provider keys. Open issues hint at what's next: a /reformat command, terminal charts for query results, and a WASI target for browser embedding.

v0.8.0 Fri, Mar 20  ·  v0.7.0 Thu, Mar 19  ·  v0.6.0 Thu, Mar 19  ·  v0.5.0 Tue, Mar 17
merged: #696 #697 #695 #684 #687
SECURITY

AI Agent Leaks pgMustard API Key Into Public PR Comment

The new \explain share pgmustard feature worked a little too well — an AI agent helpfully posted testing output, key included, to a GitHub PR.

When you teach an AI agent to share query plans with pgMustard, it might also share your API key with the world. That's what happened this week in rpg: an agent posted testing output containing PGMUSTARD_API_KEY into a public PR comment. @NikolayS spotted it quickly and rotated the key before damage was done. The lesson is blunt: agents posting to public repositories need credential scanning, full stop. The feature itself — /explain share pgmustard — shipped in v0.8.0 and works fine, assuming your helpers know what to keep quiet.

v0.8.0 Fri, Mar 20  ·  v0.7.0 Thu, Mar 19  ·  v0.6.0 Thu, Mar 19  ·  v0.5.0 Tue, Mar 17
merged: #696 #697 #695 #684 #687
FEATURE

sqlever Gets a Static Analysis Engine With 21 Safety Rules

The Sqitch-compatible migration tool now scans DDL and DML for zero-downtime hazards before they hit production.

Fifty-nine commits, ten merged PRs, and sqlever went from a migration runner to something with opinions. The analysis engine landed in #64 with a rule registry, preprocessor, and suppression system. Rules SA001 through SA010 cover DDL safety (#65); SA011 through SA021 add DML and locking checks (#72). A standalone sqlever analyze command (#70) lets teams lint migration files without deploying. The deploy command itself (#63), verify (#69), status (#62), and plan (#61) all merged this week. Open issues suggest real-world testing is imminent: mid-deploy Sqitch handoffs, end-to-end binary verification, and analysis against actual SQL files.

merged: #72 #71 #70 #63 #69

Next week's forecast: more releases, fewer leaked secrets, and at least one Lua script nobody asked for. generated 2026-03-21
the dispatch — Vol. 1, No. 2 March 15 – March 21, 2026
PENDING

USDT Tracepoints for PostgreSQL Wait Events Surface as Proof of Concept

A new PR proposes static tracepoints that would let DTrace and BPF tools observe Postgres wait events without polling.

@NikolayS opened #18 on the postgres mirror this week — a proof-of-concept adding USDT static tracepoints for wait event tracing. The approach would give DTrace and BPF-based tools direct hooks into PostgreSQL's wait event machinery, replacing the sampling-based workarounds that tools like pg_ash currently rely on. Sixty-three commits hit the fork. The PR remains open.

1 open PR: #18 PoC: USDT static tracepoints for wait event tracing
COMMUNITY

pg_ash Gets Housekeeping: Dependabot, Security Policy, Badge Fixes

The Active Session History extension receives maintenance attention between the bigger battles.

No features, no drama — just responsible stewardship. @NikolayS added a SECURITY.md policy (#24), enabled Dependabot for GitHub Actions version updates (#23), and fixed badge counts and documentation wording (#26). Dependabot promptly bumped actions/checkout from 4 to 6 (#25). Four commits, four merged PRs. Clean.

merged: #26 #24 #23 #25
RELEASE

Dental Office Intercom App Quietly Buzzes to v1.3.1

The LAN-only Android tablet buzzer system ships two debug builds in three days, because even dentists deserve version numbers.

Two releases — v1.2.5 on March 15 and v1.3.1 on March 17 — both debug builds for direct Android tablet installation. The version is now visible in the bottom-right corner of the app. One commit. No PRs. Just a dentist's office getting slightly more organized.

v1.3.1 Tue, Mar 17  ·  v1.2.5 Sun, Mar 15
